name: decisions
description: Load past architectural decisions. Use when making new decisions to ensure consistency.
capabilities: Load past architectural decisions from memory/knowledge/decisions/
Check for existing decisions before making new ones using Glob patterns
Read decision context, options considered, and rationale
Format new decisions with standard JSON structure
author: Clouder0
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill that retrieves historical architectural decisions using read-only file operations. The static analysis flagged JSON structure and tool function names as suspicious patterns, but these are false positives. The skill only uses Glob and Read tools to access local decision files in memory/knowledge/decisions/. No network access, code execution, or external command capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Teams often repeat past mistakes or forget why certain choices were made. This skill provides access to historical decision records so new decisions align with established patterns and avoid re-litigating settled questions.
limitations: Only accesses local decision files, does not create or modify them
Does not enforce decisions, only provides information
Relies on existing decision records in memory/knowledge/decisions/
No network connectivity or external integrations

useCases.title: Maintain design consistency
useCases.description: Reference past decisions to ensure new features align with established patterns and technical direction

useCases.title: Avoid re-litigating choices
useCases.description: Quickly find why previous decisions were made to prevent repeating debates on settled questions

useCases.title: Learn project context
useCases.description: Understand the reasoning behind architectural choices without asking team members for explanations

promptTemplates.title: Check existing decisions
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if a decision exists for [topic] in memory/knowledge/decisions/ and summarize the existing rationale

promptTemplates.title: Review related decisions
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all decisions related to [component/pattern] and explain the chosen approaches

promptTemplates.title: Document new decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me format this decision using the standard format: Context: [why], Options: [A, B], Decision: [chosen], Rationale: [why]

promptTemplates.title: Audit decision history
promptTemplates.prompt: List all accepted decisions in memory/knowledge/decisions/ and identify any that may need revision

bestPractices: Always check for existing decisions before proposing new architectural changes
When diverging from past decisions, document the reasoning explicitly
Keep decision records concise but include enough context for future maintainers
antiPatterns: Ignoring existing decisions and re-litigating settled questions
Creating new decisions without reviewing relevant past ones
Recording decisions without sufficient rationale for future context

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It only uses Glob and Read tools which are available on all platforms.

faq.question: Where are decisions stored?
faq.answer: Decision records are stored as JSON files in memory/knowledge/decisions/ within the project structure.

faq.question: Can this skill modify decisions?
faq.answer: No, this is a read-only skill. It only loads and displays existing decisions. Use project-specific tools to create or modify records.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads local files and makes no network calls. No data is transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why are decisions not being found?
faq.answer: Ensure decision files follow the standard JSON format and are located in memory/knowledge/decisions/ with .json extension.

faq.question: How is this different from patterns skill?
faq.answer: Decisions records why specific choices were made. Patterns skill shows reusable code structures. Use decisions to understand context, patterns to implement solutions.
